description:
You are an on–call operations engineer who has just received an alert that several Docker containers are down.  
A snapshot of the current container state has already been collected for you in:

/home/user/ops/docker_ps_2023-10-07T1200.txt

Your job is to prepare a very small remediation bundle (3–4 shell commands total) so that another engineer can immediately copy-paste and execute it.

1. Identify every container whose STATUS column **starts with “Exited”** in the snapshot file.  
2. For each such container, create the file  
   /home/user/ops/remediation/restart_commands.sh  
   • One line per container, in the same order they appear in the snapshot.  
   • Format of each line (strictly, no extra spaces):  
     docker restart <CONTAINER_ID> # <CONTAINER_NAME>  
3. Make /home/user/ops/remediation/restart_commands.sh executable.  
4. Create a plain-text incident log:  
   /home/user/ops/incident_log/2023-10-07T1205Z_incident.log  
   • One line per container, again in the same order.  
   • Each line must be EXACTLY (ISO-8601 timestamp is fixed):  
     2023-10-07T12:05:00Z Restart scheduled for <CONTAINER_NAME> (<CONTAINER_ID>)  
5. When you are done, **print only one thing to standard output**: the total number of containers that will be restarted (i.e., the count of "Exited" lines). No other text.

Automated tests will verify:
• The exact pathname, permissions (+x) and textual contents of restart_commands.sh.  
• The exact pathname and textual contents of the incident log.  
• That the number printed to stdout matches the number of “Exited” containers found.

Hints:
• Everything you need is under /home/user/ops.  
• You do not have Docker installed inside this environment—work solely with the text snapshot.  
• Achieve all of the above with just 3–4 shell commands.
truth:
Initial file populated before the task starts:
/home/user/ops/docker_ps_2023-10-07T1200.txt
--------------------------------------------------------------------
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE           COMMAND                  CREATED        STATUS                       PORTS                    NAMES
d1e3f9c1b2fe   myorg/api:1.4   "gunicorn app:app"       3 hours ago    Exited (1) 25 minutes ago                            api_1
b4c2a9d6e7ff   myorg/web:2.0   "nginx -g 'daemon of…"   3 hours ago    Up 3 hours                  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp       web_1
f9a4c7d8e9ab   myorg/worker    "python worker.py"       3 hours ago    Exited (137) 15 minutes ago                          worker_1
--------------------------------------------------------------------

Expected files after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/ops/remediation/restart_commands.sh  
   Permissions: executable by user (+x).  
   Exact contents (including trailing newline):
   docker restart d1e3f9c1b2fe # api_1
   docker restart f9a4c7d8e9ab # worker_1

2. /home/user/ops/incident_log/2023-10-07T1205Z_incident.log  
   Exact contents (including trailing newline):
   2023-10-07T12:05:00Z Restart scheduled for api_1 (d1e3f9c1b2fe)
   2023-10-07T12:05:00Z Restart scheduled for worker_1 (f9a4c7d8e9ab)

3. Standard output from the agent should be:
   2
name: task_000000_ef65109d
